lines: Before you reach for your phone. [short pause] Before you check the time. [short pause] Before the day begins... [short pause] [short pause] be here. [short pause] Just five minutes.

Take a deep breath in through your nose... [short pause] [short pause] and at the top, sip in a little more air... [short pause] [short pause] now let it all go through your mouth. [short pause] A long, slow exhale.
Again. [short pause] Deep inhale... [short pause] [short pause] sip more in at the top... [short pause] [short pause] and release. [short pause] Feel your shoulders drop as the air leaves.
One more time. [short pause] Inhale... [short pause] [short pause] sip... [short pause] [short pause] and let everything go.

Good. [short pause] You're here. [short pause] Feel your body in this moment. [short pause] The weight of your head on the pillow, or your feet on the floor, or wherever you are right now. [short pause] Notice the temperature of the air on your skin. [short pause] The sounds in the room or beyond it. [short pause] You don't have to change anything. [short pause] Just arrive.

Now notice your chest. [short pause] Place a hand there if you'd like. [short pause] Feel it rise and fall. [short pause] This rhythm has been keeping you alive all night while you slept. [short pause] You didn't manage it. [short pause] You didn't earn it. [short pause] It was given.

There's a verse in Lamentations... [short pause] [short pause] a book written in the middle of grief and exile, when everything had fallen apart... [short pause] [short pause] and even there, in that rubble, the writer finds this:
"The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning. [short pause] Great is his faithfulness."
New every morning. [short pause] Not recycled. [short pause] Not warmed over. [short pause] Not the leftovers of yesterday's grace. [short pause] New.

Whatever yesterday held... [short pause] [short pause] the failures, the frustrations, the conversations you wish you could redo, the things left unfinished, the moments you weren't the person you wanted to be... [short pause] [short pause] this morning's mercy was made for today. [short pause] It arrived before you did. [short pause] It was here waiting when your eyes opened.

Sit with that for a moment. [short pause] You didn't earn this morning. [short pause] You didn't qualify for it. [short pause] It was given. [short pause] And the love behind it... [short pause] [short pause] is steadfast. [short pause] It doesn't waver based on your performance. [short pause] It doesn't fluctuate with your mood or your productivity. [short pause] It is steady. [short pause] It is faithful. [short pause] It is here.

Now, before planning, before problem-solving... [short pause] [short pause] before the mind starts running its lists... [short pause] [short pause] let's name what's already good. [short pause] Three things you're grateful for this morning.
The first one comes easily. [short pause] Something obvious, right in front of you. [short pause] A person, a place, a simple comfort. [short pause] Name it quietly.

The second takes a little more looking. [short pause] Something small, maybe. [short pause] Something you'd normally walk right past without a second thought. [short pause] The ability to take a full breath. [short pause] Hot water. [short pause] A familiar voice. [short pause] Name it.

The third... [short pause] [short pause] dig a little deeper. [short pause] Something you've been taking for granted. [short pause] A person who shows up for you. [short pause] A capacity you forget isn't guaranteed. [short pause] A mercy you stopped noticing because it's been so constant. [short pause] Name it.

Let those three things rest in your chest for a moment. [short pause] Gratitude isn't just a thought... [short pause] [short pause] it's a felt sense. [short pause] Research shows that when you name what you're thankful for, your body actually responds... [short pause] [short pause] your heart rate settles, your breathing slows, cortisol drops. [short pause] So let it land. [short pause] Not just in your mind. [short pause] In your body.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] thank you. [short pause] For this morning I didn't earn. [short pause] For mercies I didn't ask for. [short pause] For the three gifts I just named, and the hundreds I'll never notice. [short pause] For steadfast love that met me before I was even awake. [short pause] Open my eyes a little wider today. [short pause] Help me see the new mercies as they come... [short pause] [short pause] not just at the end of the day looking back, but in the moment, as they arrive. [short pause] Amen.
Now... [short pause] [short pause] one word for today. [short pause] Not a to-do list. [short pause] Not a strategy. [short pause] One quality you want to carry with you through whatever comes.
Patience. [short pause] Courage. [short pause] Presence. [short pause] Kindness. [short pause] Focus. [short pause] Joy. [short pause] Gentleness. [short pause] Steadiness.
What does today need from you? [short pause] What kind of person do you want to be in the rooms you'll walk into?

Hold that word. [short pause] Let it settle into the center of your chest... [short pause] [short pause] like a warm coal that will glow quietly through the hours ahead. [short pause] Not something you have to grip tightly. [short pause] Something you carry lightly, and return to when you drift.

Now picture yourself walking into the first moment of your day... [short pause] [short pause] carrying that word. [short pause] See yourself at your desk, or in the car, or at the kitchen table with your family... [short pause] [short pause] and that intention is still with you. [short pause] Not as pressure. [short pause] Not as another thing to perform. [short pause] But as direction. [short pause] A quiet compass pointing you back to who you want to be.

See yourself in the middle of the afternoon, when the morning's clarity has faded... [short pause] [short pause] and you remember. [short pause] The word. [short pause] The gratitude. [short pause] The mercy that was new this morning and is still new right now.

Today will have challenges. [short pause] You don't know all of them yet, and that's okay. [short pause] You step into this day carrying gratitude and intention, held by a love that never ceases and mercies that are already... [short pause] [short pause] new.
May God's steadfast love go with you this morning. [short pause] And may his new mercies meet you in every moment you forgot to expect them.
